figures.caption: Figure 3: Overall training strategy of RCoT-Seg. Following a CoT-SFT cold-start, we employ GRPO reinforcement learning on the model using reward functions tailored to different tasks.
Figure 8: Prompt templates for AKS and KTG tasks.
Figure 16: More qualitative comparison on the ReasonVOS dataset.
Figure 17: More qualitative comparison on the ReVOS dataset.
Figure 1: Comparison between RCoT-Seg and previous methods. RCoT-Seg segments the correct target through explicit CoT reasoning and concise keyframe selection.
Table 1: Performance comparison on VRS datasets. The best and second-best results are shown in red and blue color.
Figure 7: Composition of the training datasets and sample examples.
Figure 13: Curve of CoT-SFT training loss.
Figure 5: Prompt templates for CoT datasets generation.
Table 2: Performance comparison on RVOS datasets. The best and second-best results are shown in red and blue color.
Figure 2: Overview of RCoT-Seg unified multi-task pipeline for VRS task.

Figure 14: Visualization of failure cases on the ReVOS dataset. RCoT-Seg reveals inconsistencies between its reasoning traces and final segmentation outputs, and demonstrates limited adaptability in the zero-target task.
Figure 10: Curves of timing analysis on VRS and RVOS datasets.

Figure 4: Qualitative comparison between RCoT-Seg and current SOTA methods. Our method successfully identify and accurately segment targets in complex scenarios involving small-scale objects and multi-object interactions.
Figure 15: Visualization of failure cases on the ReVOS dataset. In this case, RCoT-Seg erroneously classifies an invalid keyframe as valid in the AKS task.
Table 8: Performance comparison on VRS datasets. The best and second-best results are shown in red and blue color.
Table 9: Performance comparison on RVOS datasets. The best and second-best results are shown in red and blue color.
